channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal Government Declares Good Friday and Easter Monday as Public Holidays
item.description: The Federal Government don declare say tomorrow wey be Friday and Monday, April 6, 2026, go be public holidays to celebrate this year Easter. Minister of Interior, Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, announce am on b...

item.title: MP Urges Governors to Submit Extortion Evidence to Authorities Instead of Public Allegations
item.description: Suba South MP Caroli Omondi has advised Kenyan governors who are claiming to have been extorted to take their evidence to investigative agencies rather than making public accusations. Speaking on a lo...

item.title: Four individuals arrested in connection with arson attack on Jewish charity ambulances in London
item.description: A fourth individual has been arrested in connection with the arson attack on ambulances owned by a Jewish charity in north London, the Crown Prosecution Service has confirmed. The arrest occurred on S...

item.title: William and Kate keep distance from Beatrice and Eugenie amid Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor controversy
item.description: Prince William and Princess Kate have deliberately distanced themselves from Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie due to the ongoing Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor scandal. The princesses, daughters of the dis...

item.title: Heart Disease Risk in Men Begins in Early Thirties, Study Finds
item.description: Recent research published in the Journal of American Heart Association highlights that men’s risk for heart disease begins earlier than commonly perceived. Tracking over 5,000 adults for more than 30 ...

item.title: Community Shelters Help Women Work and Protect Children While Restoring Niger Lands
item.description: For Niger wey dey hot like oven, women wey dey try earn small money dey face wahala as dem get to care for pikin dem under strong sun. The Integrated Landscape Management Project (PGIP), wey World Ban...

item.title: Lesufi defends BEE against misleading criticisms and historical misunderstandings
item.description: Panyaza Lesufi argues that much of the debate against Black Economic Empowerment (BEE) in South Africa is not conducted in good faith. Critics often portray BEE as an irrational policy that harms econ...

item.title: India clarifies no payment issues with Iran as crude oil shipment destination changes
item.description: India on Saturday clarified that there are no payment issues with Iran regarding crude oil imports, dismissing recent reports claiming otherwise. The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas stated that ...

item.title: UK government to overhaul sofa fire safety regulations over health concerns
item.description: British fire safety regulations for sofas, in place for nearly forty years, have required furniture to be treated with significant amounts of chemical flame retardants to pass fire tests. Campaigners ...

item.title: DIG Margaret Ochalla Allegedly Intervenes in Fraud Case Involving Happyland Estate Executives
item.description: According to multiple sources, Nwankwo and Omenal, whistleblowers from Happyland Estate in Lagos, were allegedly arrested and taken to Abuja under controversial circumstances after raising alarm about...

item.title: Raghav Chadha and AAP Punjab clash over parliamentary issues
item.description: Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha has strongly rebutted allegations from his own party colleagues, asserting that he is being targeted through a coordinated campaign. Following his removal as deputy leader...

item.title: Farouk Kibet Urges Former President Uhuru Kenyatta to Respect Retirement and Avoid Politics
item.description: Allies of President William Ruto, notably Farouk Kibet, have publicly called on former President Uhuru Kenyatta to retire fully from politics and allow the current administration to function without i...

item.title: M&S Leadership Calls for Stronger Police Action Amid Rising Retail Crime
item.description: Marks & Spencer's retail director, Thinus Keeve, has expressed serious concerns over rising crime in London, particularly retail-targeted incidents by groups of young people coordinating through socia...

item.title: Parliament panel urges comprehensive counting of all disabilities in Census 2027
item.description: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Social Justice and Empowerment has stressed the need for a precise and comprehensive database of persons with disabilities (PwDs) in India. Highlighting that th...

item.title: Nairobi City Thunder Eye Wins to Boost Playoff Hopes in BAL Kalahari Conference
item.description: Nairobi City Thunder are gearing up for a crucial match against Libyan champions Al Ahly Ly in the Basketball Africa League (BAL) Kalahari Conference, scheduled for Friday evening at the SunBet Arena....

item.title: Toronto Italians invited to swap jerseys for Canada's 2026 World Cup support
item.description: On Saturday, Toronto's Little Italy neighbourhood saw a unique initiative from Canada Soccer, inviting Italian-Canadian fans to exchange their Italy jerseys for Canada's new red home shirts in support...

item.title: Notable Quotes from Henry David Thoreau Highlighted in New Documentary
item.description: A new three-part PBS documentary brings renewed attention to the 19th-century writer and naturalist Henry David Thoreau. Featuring a star-studded cast including Jeff Goldblum as Thoreau and narration ...

item.title: The Challenges of Excelling in Test Cricket Compared to T20
item.description: Ramachandra Guha reflects on the challenges of being a successful Test cricketer versus excelling in T20 cricket. He draws on Scyld Berry's book, '500 Declared', which chronicles the experience of cov...

item.title: Pakistan clarifies UAE financial deposits return is routine transaction
item.description: On Saturday, the Foreign Office (FO) clarified that reports suggesting any controversy around financial deposits from the United Arab Emirates (UAE) were misleading and unfounded. A senior Pakistani o...

item.title: Fiscal Pressure, Health Systems, and Local Government Responsibilities in the Philippines
item.description: The article examines how historical debt crises in the Philippines, particularly during the early 1980s under the Marcos administration, directly impacted public health systems. Despite apparent incre...
